title: aconogonon alaskanum subsp. hultenianum
intro: Aconogonon alaskanum subsp. hultenianum is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae, commonly known as buckwheat or knotweed. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Aconogonon alaskanum subsp. hultenianum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves or roots in water, and can be used as a tea, tincture, or poultice to harness its medicinal properties.
